name: The Clash at Black Spire Ridge
description:
World of Teravas

The Clash at Black Spire Ridge

Filed by: Field Archivist Thallian Orrex, Corvidian Office of War Chronicles
Date: 17th of Ashfall, 7th Cycle, Year of the Winged Eclipse
Location: Black Spire Ridge, Border of the Ashglass Expanse
Security Level: Officer Review Only

Summary:

The 4th Blackwing Crusade Battalion, under Canon-Militant Erodan Vael, engaged with forces of the Cackling Dominion at Black Spire Ridge. While initially believed to be a routine skirmish, the battle resulted in a devastating rout for the Corvidian side, with the majority of the battalion lost in a matter of hours.

The battle has exposed the Cackling Dominion’s increasing tactical discipline and their use of psychological and ceremonial displays to undermine morale.

Pre-Engagement Context:

  • Terrain: Elevated ridgeline with sharp crags, few trees, and a series of old Corvidian watchposts.

  • Weather: Fog-laden morning with light ashfall from Mount Vareth.

  • Objective: Reclamation and reconsecration of the Shrine of Saint Ulrix’s Gaze, overtaken by Dominion scouts.

Battle Overview:

07:00 Hours – Corvidian forces begin a formal march toward the ridge, flanked by Light Talon sharpshooters and supported by two mobile cannon crews.

07:45 – Cackling Dominion forces, dressed in a mix of tribal finery and captured uniforms, begin advancing in loose, irregular formations—drumbeats echo through the mist. Their approach is loud and theatrical, intended to disorient and unnerve.

08:00 – First volley exchanged. The Blackwing line holds firm, but Dominion skirmishers flank from the west under cover of smoke.

08:15 – Dominion forces execute an unexpected pincer maneuver, using the terrain and ceremonial drums to mislead troop movement expectations.

08:40 – Canon-Militant Vael orders a retreat to higher ground near the Shrine. Dominion cavalry—mounted on ash-deer—disrupt the formation, causing a breakdown of command cohesion.

09:10 – Final Corvidian resistance collapses as ammunition stores run dry. Survivors report non-lethal capture of a small number of officers, presumably for negotiation or messaging purposes.

Aftermath:

  • Estimated Casualties: 284 Corvidian (mostly from miscommunication and encirclement), 37 wounded, 4 confirmed captured.

  • Dominion forces do not occupy the site but instead retreat after ceremonial flag placement and symbolic markings on the shrine.

No defilement of remains was observed. Instead, captured officers’ weapons and hats were neatly stacked and left at the base of the shrine—a silent, unsettling message.

Observations:

  • The Dominion appears to blend ritualized theatrics with battlefield cunning, mirroring Napoleonic formations when convenient but abandoning them for skirmisher freedom when it suits their terrain.

  • Psychological tactics are subtle but effective—mock salutes, exaggerated marching, and coded drum signals were used to confuse and misdirect.

  • The Corvidian reliance on divine timing and tight formations left us vulnerable to lateral maneuvers.

Recommendations:

  • Begin immediate review of anti-flanking doctrine for Shrine defense units.

  • Incorporate counter-drum tactics an
creator: Riko Travis
